name: Internal Consistency
trigger: A new component, pattern, label, or interaction is being introduced and could either reuse an existing product convention or diverge from it.
recommendation: Keep the same things looking and behaving the same way throughout the product: reuse established components, spacing, terminology, icon meanings, and interaction patterns so a behaviour learned in one place transfers everywhere. Centralise decisions in a design system and deviate only with deliberate, documented reason.
rejection: Reject multiple bespoke versions of the same control, the same concept named differently across screens, primary buttons that look different per page, and one-off interaction patterns that duplicate an existing solved problem.
validation: Audit for duplicate or divergent components solving the same problem and consolidate
Check terminology for the same concept is identical across the product
Confirm new patterns either reuse the system or are added to it with rationale
evidence: Nielsen Norman Group Heuristic 4: Consistency and Standards
Internal consistency reduces learning cost; external consistency (Jakob's Law) reduces it further
